sglang.title: [Docs] quick fix delete --enable-dp-attention in sgl-jax
sglang.author: JamesBrianD
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
In sgl-jax, DP attention is enabled by default and the --enable-dp-attention flag does not exist. The MiMo-V2.5-Pro TPU launch command in the cookbook included this flag, which would cause the server to fail at argument parsing.
Modifications
docs_new/src/snippets/autoregressive/mimo-v25-

sglang.title: fix(lora): avoid CUDA graph-breaking scalar assignment in seg_indptr
sglang.author: yushengsu-thu
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Replace sgemm.seg_indptr[0] = 0 with sgemm.seg_indptr[0:1].zero_() to prevent a CPU-GPU sync point that breaks CUDA graph capture. The scalar assignment triggers a host-to-device copy which is incompatible with CUDA graph recording, while the slice-based .zero_() stays entirely on-device.
Mitigate t

sglang.author: opherlieber
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Requests with total length prompt_length+max_new_tokens=context_len should be allowed. This is exactly what the auto-truncate code below truncates to, and also the same way VLLM checks.

Modifications
Allow requests with exactly context_len total tokens.

Speed Tests and P

Sibling labels split capacity. A single B200 host advertises 4-gpu-b200, 4-gpu-b200-kernel, 4-gpu-b200-low-disk, 4-gpu-b200-kernel-low-disk — the old denominator put it

sglang.title: Fix KeyError in prepare_lora_batch when lora_ids contains None
sglang.author: erikwijmans
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
CUDA graph padding in _pad_inputs_to_size extends lora_ids with None entries. These padding entries are never loaded into the memory pool, so get_buffer_id raises KeyError when it encounters them.
Modifications
The fix guards with uid not in self.memory_pool.uid_to_buffer_id instead of ui

LFM2 uses a hybrid cache:

request/token pool indices identify request rows
Mamba/short-conv state indices identify persistent convolution state rows

These are separate namespaces. The LFM2 short-conv path was using

The inter-chunk kernel (chunk_gated_delta_rule_fwd_h) stores h in (V, K) layout (FLA's TRANSPOSE_STATE=True mode), but the output kernel was loading it as (K, V), re

vllm.title: [KVConnector] MultiConnector SupportsHMA
vllm.author: NickLucche
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds support for MultiConnector to handle subconnectors that implement the SupportsHMA by implementing the interface as well.
The connector will fall back to "single-group" mode when any of the connectors do not support hma, while asserting that the model doesn't necessitate more.
Will share

vllm.title: [P/D] Prefill compute optimizations with bi-directional KV cache transfers between P and D nodes
vllm.author: snadampal
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Prefill worker can pull KV cache from remote engines to eliminate redundant prefill computation
Benefits:
(1) Multi-turn conversations: Prefill worker loads larger KV cache from decode worker
(2) Cache eviction recovery: Prefill worker retrieves evicted blocks still available on decode worker
Purpos

The NIXL EPLB communicator previously allocated local_moe_layer_size * ep_size for its send buffer to handle the worst case where a rank sends all its experts to every peer. For DeepSeek-V3.2 with EP=8 this added ~14 GiB of overhead.
This PR shrinks the send buffer to local_moe_layer_size by

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix RoutedExpertsCapturer for Gemma 4 MoE (top_k_experts)
vllm.author: lequytra
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
RoutedExpertsCapturer and RoutedExpertsReader hard-code hf_config.num_experts_per_tok when sizing the shared-memory capture buffers. Gemma 4 (Gemma4TextConfig) stores this value under top_k_experts instead, causing an AttributeError when --enable-return-routed-experts is set.

Add a _get_num

vllm.title: [DeepSeek] Use torch.mm for bf16xbf16->fp32 gemm
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
It turns out that torch.mm natively supports bf16xbf16->fp32 gemm, so we don't need to wrap cublas ourselves. The following script confirms that nvjet_sm100_tss_16x64_64x16_4x1_v_bz_TNN is being launched under the hood.
import torch
import torch.nn as nn


with torch.inference_mode():
    import tor

vllm.title: [Core] Simplify handling of `scheduler_reserve_full_isl` option
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Minor follow-on from #37307 to simplify the changes and reduce scheduler-side logic.

vllm.title: (bugfix): block_size check for flex attn
vllm.author: JisoLya
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Closes #41339
Description
Previously, when a user set block_size < 16 (e.g., block_size=8), vLLM would trigger a cryptic Triton compilation error during the runtime, which was difficult for users to debug and understand.
Changes
Added block_size compatibility checks for flex attention backen

vllm.title: [ROCm][CI] Add ROCm score absolute tolerance floor
vllm.author: AndreasKaratzas
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR keeps the existing per-backend relative tolerances for the online vision cross-encoder score tests, but adds a small backend-specific absolute tolerance floor for ROCM_AITER_FA and FLEX_ATTENTION.
The affected failures are low-probability text-vs-text scores on ROCm 7.2/gfx950. Those scores

vllm.title: feat: Enable `prompt_embeds` Content Part Support in vLLM Chat Completions API
vllm.author: LuisRobaina
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
vLLM supports prompt_embeds (pre-computed embeddings) in the Completions API (/v1/completions) via the --enable-prompt-embeds flag. This allows users to bypass the model's embedding layer by providing a serialized tensor of shape (num_tokens, hidden_size).
However, the Chat Completions API (

vllm.title: [Model] Add Moondream3 model support(only query and caption skills)
vllm.author: sniper35
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Closes #25215.
Implementation
In this PR, only query and caption skills are implemented out of the four kills in the original HF repo:  query, caption, point, detect. Refer to the notes at the end about why point, detect skills are not implemented this time.

In the current Moondream3 implem

vllm.title: [KV Offload] Use `Collection` instead of `Sequence/Iterable` for OffloadingManager key parameters
vllm.author: ronensc
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Follow-up to #41200.
OffloadingManager method signatures used Sequence[OffloadKey] where no implementation indexes by position, and Iterable[OffloadKey] where keys is iterated more than once (e.g. prepare_store).
complete_load/complete_store callers already pass set[OffloadKey], which isn't

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Disable FlashInfer CUTLASS MoE on SM110 (Jetson Thor AGX)
vllm.author: stecasta
vllm.bodyExcerpt: flashinfer <= 0.6.8.post1 ships no SM110 MoE cubins. On SM110 the SM100 artifact is reused at runtime and the TMA-WS dispatcher picks tile 256x256x128 which has no SM100 registration, raising Unsupported tile shape config 256256128 for MoE gemm at moe_gemm_template_dispatch_tma_ws.h:486 when running

vllm.title: [Kernel][MoE] Support GELU on TRT-LLM NvFP4 fused MoE for Gemma4
vllm.author: juhi10071998
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Enables MoEActivation.GELU (gated GELU, GeGLU) on the FlashInfer TRT-LLM NvFP4 MoE backend (TrtLlmNvFp4Experts* in  vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/experts/trtllm_nvfp4_moe.py). The  GPU kernel and the dispatcher already support GeGLU end-to-end — only the Python allow-list and assertio

vllm.title: [kv_offload+HMA][12/N]: Scheduler-side support for sliding window groups
vllm.author: orozery
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR extends the scheduler-side OffloadingConnector to support sliding window and Mamba KV cache groups.
Tests will be added in the next (and final) PR which will add SupportsHMA to the offloading connector.

vllm.title: Add nvfp4 kv cache support
vllm.author: sychen52
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This is a follow up of #37332 to fully enable nvfp4 kv cache using flashinfer trtllm backend.
This builds on the existing NVFP4 infrastructure (packed layout, reshape_and_cache_flash kernel, KVQuantMode.NVFP4) and wires up the read/attention side so that end-to-end NVFP4 KV cache inference works thr

vllm.title: [compile] Add FlashInfer FP8 async TP fusion and preserve allreduce fusion ordering #27893
vllm.author: baonudesifeizhai
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
#27893
This PR enables AsyncTP fusion for FlashInfer FP8 GEMMs by rewriting:

all_gather + vllm.bmm_fp8
vllm.bmm_fp8 + reduce_scatter

into SymmetricMemory-backed fused ops using bmm_fp8_out.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Pass reasoning parser kwargs to structured output
vllm.author: BugenZhao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fixes #41132. Fixed #33215.
When structured output is combined with reasoning parsers, the v1 engine-side structured output gate needs to instantiate the reasoning parser with the same chat-template kwargs used by the frontend prompt renderer. This matters for parsers whose behavior depends
